meaning: To add more detail or information about something.
examples: Could you elaborate on your proposal?
She elaborated her plans during the presentation.

meaning: Easy to understand or simple; not complicated.
examples: The instructions were straightforward and easy to follow.
It was a straightforward decision.

meaning: To communicate or express something, with or without words.
examples: He struggled to convey his ideas clearly.
The report conveys the urgency of the situation.

meaning: Without being affected by something; in spite of something.
examples: We'll go ahead with the plan regardless of the weather.
She continued working regardless of the interruptions.

meaning: Real, exactly what it appears to be; sincere and honest.
examples: Her concern for the team was genuine.
That's a genuine leather bag.

meaning: Done carefully and completely, covering every detail.
examples: She did a thorough review of the document.
The investigation was very thorough.

meaning: Despite what has just been said or referred to.
examples: It was raining; nevertheless, we went for a walk.
The task was difficult. Nevertheless, she completed it.

meaning: To have a very strong emotional effect on someone; to be too much to deal with.
examples: She was overwhelmed by the support from her colleagues.
Don't overwhelm yourself with too many tasks.

meaning: Not immediately obvious or noticeable; delicate.
examples: There's a subtle difference between the two designs.
His humor is very subtle.

meaning: Exactly; in a way that is accurate and careful.
examples: That's precisely what I meant.
The meeting starts at precisely 3 PM.

meaning: To accept or admit the existence or truth of something.
examples: She acknowledged the mistake and moved on.
He acknowledged the team's hard work.

meaning: To suggest something without saying it directly.
examples: Are you implying that I made an error?
Her tone implied she was not happy with the result.

meaning: To give a particular job, duty, or responsibility to someone else.
examples: A good manager knows how to delegate tasks effectively.
She delegated the report to her assistant.

meaning: To decide which tasks are most important and deal with them first.
examples: We need to prioritize our tasks before the deadline.
Learning to prioritize is essential for productivity.

meaning: To work jointly with others on an activity or project.
examples: Our teams collaborated on the product launch.
She collaborated with engineers to solve the issue.

meaning: To become or make something become greater or more serious.
examples: If the issue persists, escalate it to the manager.
Tensions escalated during the negotiation.

meaning: To make an action or process easier or more likely to happen.
examples: She facilitated the discussion between departments.
Technology facilitates remote work.

meaning: To put a plan or system into operation.
examples: We plan to implement the new policy next quarter.
The team implemented the changes ahead of schedule.

meaning: To integrate a new employee into an organization.
examples: We need to onboard three new hires this week.
The onboarding process takes about two weeks.

meaning: To bring into agreement or cooperation with a particular group or position.
examples: Let's align our goals before the next sprint.
The departments need to align on the strategy.

meaning: Information about reactions to a product or person's performance, used as a basis for improvement.
examples: I'd appreciate your feedback on this draft.
Constructive feedback helps the team grow.

meaning: The latest time or date by which something should be completed.
examples: Can we extend the deadline by one day?
Meeting deadlines is crucial for client trust.

meaning: A person or group with an interest or concern in something.
examples: We presented the results to key stakeholders.
All stakeholders should be informed about the change.

meaning: To take further action on something or contact someone again.
examples: I'll follow up with the client tomorrow.
Please follow up on the pending tasks.

meaning: To make people feel more comfortable in a social situation.
examples: He told a joke to break the ice at the meeting.
Games helped break the ice between teams.

meaning: To start an activity or process.
examples: Let's get the ball rolling on the new project.
Who wants to get the ball rolling with ideas?

meaning: To have the same understanding or opinion about something.
examples: Let's make sure we're on the same page before the meeting.
The team needs to be on the same page about the deadline.

meaning: To think creatively or in an innovative way.
examples: We need to think outside the box to solve this problem.
Her ability to think outside the box impressed the team.

meaning: To start something and proceed at a fast pace with enthusiasm.
examples: The new hire hit the ground running on day one.
We need to hit the ground running after the holiday break.

meaning: To make more effort than is expected of you.
examples: She always goes the extra mile for her clients.
Going the extra mile can set you apart at work.

meaning: To do something in the easiest or cheapest way, often sacrificing quality.
examples: Don't cut corners on safety procedures.
They cut corners to meet the deadline.

meaning: To keep someone informed about what is happening.
examples: Please keep me in the loop on the project status.
It's important to keep stakeholders in the loop.

meaning: To make contact or communicate briefly with someone.
examples: Let's touch base next week to review progress.
I wanted to touch base before the presentation.

text: Bite off more than you can chew
meaning: To try to do more than you are able to do.
examples: He bit off more than he could chew with three projects.
Be careful not to bite off more than you can chew.

meaning: To decide to stop working on something.
examples: We've made good progress. Let's call it a day.
After eight hours, she decided to call it a day.

meaning: To start planning something again because the previous attempt failed.
examples: The proposal was rejected, so it's back to the drawing board.
We need to go back to the drawing board on this design.

meaning: To perform better than someone or something.
examples: Our team outperformed the sales target this quarter.
This laptop outperforms the previous model in speed.

meaning: To use something to maximum advantage.
examples: We should leverage our existing customer base for growth.
The company leveraged technology to reduce costs.

meaning: A standard or point of reference against which things may be compared.
examples: We use industry benchmarks to measure our performance.
This product sets a new benchmark for quality.

meaning: To make a system or organization more efficient by simplifying it.
examples: We streamlined the onboarding process to save time.
The workflow was streamlined to remove bottlenecks.

meaning: Able to be changed in size or scale to meet demand.
examples: We need a scalable solution for our growing user base.
The architecture was designed to be scalable.

meaning: To make the best or most effective use of a situation or resource.
examples: We optimized the algorithm for faster processing.
The team worked to optimize the customer experience.

meaning: To make something less severe, serious, or painful.
examples: We took steps to mitigate the risk of data loss.
The new policy helps mitigate potential issues.

meaning: Creating or controlling a situation rather than just responding to it.
examples: A proactive approach to problem-solving saves time.
She is proactive about identifying risks early.

meaning: To repeat a process in order to generate a sequence of outcomes or to make improvements.
examples: We iterated on the design based on user feedback.
The team plans to iterate quickly on the prototype.

meaning: Introducing new ideas or methods; original and creative.
examples: The company is known for its innovative products.
We encourage innovative thinking in our team.

meaning: Able to recover quickly from difficult conditions.
examples: A resilient team adapts to change quickly.
The system is resilient to network failures.

meaning: The combined effect of working together that is greater than individual efforts.
examples: The merger created synergy between the two companies.
There's great synergy between the design and engineering teams.
